what_manipulated: latent classes of narcissism: admiration vs. rivalry pathways
what_measured: personality traits and adjustment outcomes: self-esteem, empathy, interpersonal distrust
experiment: Latent class analysis identified subgroups via item response patterns on two narcissism subscales (admiration, rivalry); relatedness to personality traits and adjustment indicators tested via latent class regression and CFA with class membership as predictor.

what_manipulated: level of processing (perceptual vs. semantic) and musical prime valence (happy vs. angry) during encoding
what_measured: incidental recognition memory for emotion words (joy, anger, disgust, neutral)
experiment: Between-subjects design crossed processing depth with music type; participants encoded words via graphemic or semantic task while music played; surprise recognition memory tested with hits minus false alarms corrected.

what_measured: perceived stress at 3-month follow-up; social support from recipients' and providers' perspectives (baseline)
experiment: Longitudinal dyadic design; optimism predicted stress prospectively; actor-partner interdependence models tested whether recipients' perceived support and providers' reported support mediated the optimism-stress relationship.

what_manipulated: informal competition (perceived obstacle level) and contextual moderators: formal competition, irregular payment prevalence, regulatory optimism
what_measured: new product development: binary outcome (introduced new or upgraded product vs. did not)
experiment: Large cross-national survey of 9,522 firms; logistic regression with country fixed effects tested whether informal competition predicts NPD; main effect moderated by formal competition, corruption prevalence, and managerial optimism about regulatory environment.

what_manipulated: dictator game frame: giving (dictator owns endowment, gives to recipient) vs. taking (recipient owns endowment, dictator takes)
what_measured: amount allocated to recipient; social type classification (selfish vs. egalitarian)
experiment: Between-subjects design with 70 dictators and 70 recipients per frame; dictators allocated £0-£10 under neutral framing; recognition measured by hits/false-alarms corrected. Gender-stratified analyses and Probit models tested framing effects on social type.

what_manipulated: group size (2 vs. 5 players), player position (upstream vs. downstream), and download interruption (files preserved vs. lost if upstream blocks bandwidth)
what_measured: infrastructure investment (tokens), resource extraction (files downloaded), earnings, income inequality (Gini coefficient)
experiment: Laboratory commons dilemma with two decisions: investment in shared bandwidth, then extraction via file downloads. Real-time interaction with sequential access asymmetry; between-subjects treatments, 10 rounds with communication; hierarchical linear models and group-level regression analyzed investment and extraction by position and round.
